channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: RocheDB v0.6.0: Locality Validation, Topology Remapping, and Safer Query Boundaries
item.description: I released RocheDB v0.6.0. Release: https://github.com/puffball1567/rochedb/releases/tag/v0.6.0 RocheDB is a ring-oriented NoSQL document and vector database written in Nim. The project is still a...

item.title: I sent an AI agent to buy from real stores. Here's what actually breaks.
item.description: AI agents are starting to shop for people. ChatGPT has an operator that clicks around the web. Perplexity ships a "buy" button. Anthropic and others are wiring agents into real checkout flows. If...

item.title: New NadMesh Botnet Hunts Exposed AI Services for Cloud Keys and Kubernetes Tokens
item.description: A Go botnet called NadMesh turned up in early July hunting exposed AI services, and the operator's own dashboard claims 3,811 unique AWS keys. A Shodan harvester keeps the scan queue stocked with...

item.title: From Curiosity to Community: Why I Started Omnikon
item.description: I'm a Computer Science student and the founder of Omnikon, an open-source organization built around one idea: students learn the most by building real projects with real people. Like many...

item.title: I Built an Open-Source Seller Earnings Calculator for Software Marketplaces
item.description: Selling a software product for €49 does not mean the seller earns €49. Marketplace commissions, payment-processing fees, VAT, refunds, affiliate commissions, and fixed transaction costs can reduce...

item.title: Dev Opportunity Radar #8: OpenAI Build Week, AIAF Fellowship, The Residency & AI Dev Tools Zoomcamp
item.description: TL;DR Welcome back to Dev Opportunity Radar. This is a weekly series where I share opportunities, resources, communities, and interesting finds that I come across, with the goal of helping people...

item.title: Contribute to Omnikon in GSSoC 2026
item.description: 🚀 Contribute to Omnikon in GSSoC 2026 Looking to make meaningful open-source contributions this GSSoC? We'd love to have you at Omnikon. Our organization is focused on building practical tools and...

item.title: Construindo um assistente pessoal 100% local com Ollama, LangChain e voz — e as armadilhas que ninguém conta
item.description: Construindo um JARVIS local: agentes, voz e as armadilhas dos LLMs pequenos Nos últimos dias montei um assistente pessoal do zero rodando 100% na minha máquina — sem chamadas pra API paga, sem...

item.title: Linux Foundation x402 protocol enables native AI payments
item.description: The Linux Foundation has officially launched the x402 Foundation to oversee a new open standard for digital transactions. This protocol allows artificial intelligence agents, applications, and...

item.title: Modern Compute Strategies for Agentic Workloads
item.description: The rapid rise of AI agents is fundamentally shifting how engineers think about cloud infrastructure and resource management. While Kubernetes served as the standard for container orchestration...

item.title: ISO Standardizes Classic McEliece for Post-Quantum Security
item.description: The International Organization for Standardization recently integrated the Classic McEliece algorithm into the ISO-IEC 18033-2 standard for asymmetric ciphers. This official inclusion provides a...

item.title: Asus’ top-end 4K QD-OLED gaming monitor is $400 off
item.description: The Asus ROG Swift 32-inch 4K QD-OLED gaming monitor has almost everything I want in a high-end gaming monitor, most notably a QD-OLED panel for inky black levels and vivid colors that go beyond...

item.title: GoldenEyeDog Subgroup Linked to DigiCert Breach and Code-Signing Certificate Theft
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have attributed the April 2026 DigiCert security incident to a threat activity cluster dubbed CylindricalCanine. Expel, which shared technical details of the event,...

item.title: Fine-tune video and image models at scale with NVIDIA NeMo Automodel and 🤗 Diffusers
Samsung’s redesigned Z Fold 8 with a wide display just leaked
item.description: Leaker Evan Blass shared images of Samsung's redesigned Galaxy Z Fold 8 just days before the July 22nd launch event where Samsung is expected to officially announce the phone. The leaked images...

item.title: Is America ready for this quirky Jeep-looking EV that can park itself?
item.description: Are we living through a small car renaissance? There's the Slate Truck, Amble's dune buggy, and the Fiat Topolino, as well as a whole galaxy of kei cars and trucks from Japan that have their own...

item.title: Florida man arrested for allegedly stealing over $200,000 in crypto using Steam game malware
item.description: Federal authorities have arrested a Florida man suspected of stealing at least $220,000 in crypto through malware-infected Steam games, as reported earlier by local news outlet Local10. In the...

item.title: Patreon stops asking AI bots not to scrape — and starts blocking them
item.description: Patreon is strengthening its defenses against AI scraping by working with Cloudflare to block bots that train AI models on creators’ content without permission. The move marks a shift away from...

item.title: Over half a million power tool batteries have been recalled due to a USB-C charging fire risk
item.description: Greenworks Tools has issued a recall for around 554,780 Kobalt-branded power tools designed for yard work because of a battery issue "posing a risk of serious injury from fire hazard." There have...

item.title: Bethesda teases Fallout 5 soon after Xbox’s mass layoffs
item.description: Xbox is currently in a "reset" period that includes laying off around 3,200 employees over the next year, involving deep cuts at beloved studios like id Software and Obsidian Entertainment. Now,...

item.title: The manosphere’s testosterone fever is coming for the troops
item.description: This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your life. Opt in...

item.title: Fake Coding Tests Deliver OtterCookie-Aligned Malware Hidden in SVG Flag Images
item.description: North Korean threat actors linked to the Contagious Interview campaign have been observed employing steganography in SVG image files to conceal malicious payloads as part of a campaign using fake...

item.title: Even Microsoft couldn’t make Windows 11 work well on 8GB of RAM
item.description: Last year, Microsoft's 13-inch Surface Laptop quickly became one of my favorite thin-and-light Windows notebooks. At $900, it was easy to recommend to anyone wanting MacBook Air-like build quality...

item.title: Trump is selling high-speed access to his market-moving Truth Social posts
item.description: Trump Media, the company behind Truth Social, is selling Wall Street faster access to the "most market-moving" posts on the US President's social media platform. On Thursday, Trump Media announced...

item.title: The Download: perimenopause misinformation and China’s latest AI leap
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. There’s a lot of hype around perimenopause. Don’t buy it....

item.title: Why the first GPU financiers are turning to inference chips in a $400 million deal
item.description: A $400 million chip-backed loan points to the next wave of AI infrastructure deals.

item.title: E.U. Orders Google to Open Android Mic, Camera and Screen to Rival AI Assistants
item.description: The European Commission on Thursday ordered Google to give rival AI assistants the same reach into Android that Gemini already has: the camera, the microphone, whatever is on screen, a wake word...

item.title: The Race to Field Military Autonomy Is On, Can Trusted Information Infrastructure Keep Pace?
item.description: Military forces are under increasing pressure to field autonomous capabilities faster than ever before. Across the U.S., UK, and NATO, new investment, evolving defense strategies, and accelerated...

item.title: Details of Alan Turing’s Voice Encryption System
item.description: Really interesting piece of cryptographic history: In November 2023, a large cache of his wartime papers—nicknamed the “Bayley papers”—was auctioned in London for almost half a million U.S....

item.title: The war on ‘woke science’ comes for space research
item.description: The Trump administration is waging a culture war on science, and the latest salvo is in the form of a dry, bureaucratic proposal from the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) that could threaten...
